source_pr.title: planner/core: use stable sort to make CI stable
source_pr.body: ### What problem does this PR solve? sort.Slice may return unstable data so the order of join's child may be unstable. Hence, CI's result is unstable. ### What is changed and how it works? use sort.SliceStable instead. ### Check List Tests - Unit test - Integration test --- This change is [](https://reviewable.io/reviews/pingcap/tidb/8650)

root_cause_explanation: The join reorder logic used an unstable sort; when row-count ties occurred, the join group ordering (and thus the chosen plan/EXPLAIN) could vary across runs. This PR stabilizes it by switching to sort.SliceStable to make tie-breaking deterministic.
fix_pattern: Use stable sort/deterministic tie-breakers in optimizer to avoid plan nondeterminism.

evidence: evidence: Hence, CI's result is unstable.
